howItWorks.intro: This recession probability calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Probability Score = Sum of weighted indicator scores (yield curve, unemployment, inflation, fed policy)
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: Can we predict recessions?
faq.answer: No model perfectly predicts recessions, but the inverted yield curve has preceded every US recession since 1955 with a 12-18 month lead time. This tool combines multiple indicators for a rough probability estimate.

faq.question: Is the Recession Probability Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What yield curve should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate yield curve value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

faq.question: Can I use this calculator on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes, the Recession Probability Calculator is fully responsive and works on any device — phone, tablet, or desktop. No app download needed.

sections.title: What Is the Recession Probability Calculator?
sections.body: The Recession Probability Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your yield curve, unemployment trend, inflation level, you get instant results including recession probability, expected timeframe, expected severity. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting recession probability right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your yield curve and need to find the right recession probability. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Yield Curve
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your yield curve — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: unemployment trend, inflation level, fed policy. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your recession probability, expected timeframe, expected severity. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your yield curve before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both recession probability and expected timeframe — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your fed policy, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
